Cytopus
  • Contributing
  • View page source

Contributing

This section summarizes contribution-related information available in the repository. The repository contains no explicit CONTRIBUTING.md file or contribution guide; the content below is derived from repository structure and common practice. No inferred policies are added.

How to contribute (not defined explicitly in repo)

  • The repository does not include a CONTRIBUTING.md nor does it document a branch strategy.

  • General steps to contribute (not enforced by repository files): * Fork the repository and make changes on a feature branch. * Submit a pull request with a clear description of the change.

Code style and standards

  • The repository contains Python code but does not include a style guide, linter config, or pre-commit hooks.

Tests and validation

  • There are no test suites in the repository; contributors should include unit tests where appropriate when adding functionality.

Contact and authorship

  • setup.py lists author=”Thomas Walle”. No maintainer contact information or issue templates are present in the repository.

Note about missing information

  • Because the repository does not provide formal contribution instructions, branching strategy, or code formatting rules, contributors should follow common Python packaging and contribution best practices or coordinate with maintainers externally.
